summary: Iter 69 (Subdivide oversized models) — backend regression PASS (8/8 projects CRUD). Frontend NEW flows verified end-to-end via Playwright: oversize detection toast, Subdivide dialog open from toast, header sizes, default Auto 1/1/1 grid, expected-pieces=8, +/- counters w/ live preview update, dec clamp at 0, Manual mode input + chip add/remove, connector picker (Dowels) + size slider visibility + summary label, Apply Auto+Dowels producing 8 named pieces (Cube/x1a/y1a/z1a … x1b/y1b/z1b) each with 24 vertices, original cube removed, success toast 'Subdivided into 20 pieces' (= 8 pieces + 12 dowel connectors), exploded view confirmed (positions {±8,±8,±8} on the 8 octants — 8 distinct positions), and re-detection on build-volume/printer change. All assertions held.

issues: After completing a subdivide, the announcement modal 'New ways to sign in' (V1.1 update) appeared/persisted on top of the workspace, partially obstructing further interaction in a fresh tab. Cosmetic — not blocking but disruptive during automated re-runs.

action_items: (P3) Tip carousel regression (iter 67/68) and built-in slicer Ctrl+S local-save regression (iter 66) were NOT re-verified in this iteration — main agent self-verified previously and no code changes touched those paths. Recommend a smoke check if any change near tipCarousel/Settings was made.

(P3) 'Ignore' toast de-dup for the SAME object id could not be cleanly verified in automation because Sonner toasts from earlier test phases persisted on the page and Zustand setState shortcut bypassed setPrinter's normal flow. Functionality looks correct in code (toastedSet tracks ids) but worth a manual confirm.

(P3) 'forgeslicer:frame-bbox' CustomEvent + camera target update on toast not directly asserted from automation (requires 3D scene inspection). Listener exists in Viewport.jsx — recommend a unit test or jest-dom event dispatch in a follow-up iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: subdivide-auto +/- buttons: Playwright's element.click() landed slightly off when the warning toast was floating over the dialog top region; native HTMLButtonElement.click() worked. The DOM buttons themselves are fully clickable for real users (verified by hover & screenshot), but a small top-padding inside the dialog header would push the +/- controls out of any toast overlap zone — minor UX polish.

Connectors are tagged in scene state but the canonical tag/flag name isn't documented (test filter `o.tag==='subdivideConnector' || o.subdivideConnector===true` matched 0 even though total-pieces math implies 12 are present). Consider standardising a single field (e.g. `meta.kind = 'subdivideConnector'`) and exposing it in oversizeCheck/SubdivideDialog comments for testability.
Workspace.jsx new useEffect for oversize detection is correctly debounced 350ms — confirmed.
store.js exposes `window.__forgeStore` for debug (great for testability, already used).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on verified flows (oversize-detect, dialog UI, apply, exploded view, redetect)

test_credentials: Seeded ephemeral mongo session tokens (TOKEN_A=st_iter69_A_1780167771) for the Workspace gate; deleted after run.
seed_data_creation: 1 mongo user + session inserted into test_database (users + user_sessions). Cleaned up at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use `window.__forgeStore.getState()` to interact with scene state directly. Useful actions: addPrimitive(type,'positive'), setTransform(id,'scale',[..]), removeObject(id). The store also supports setState (zustand) for forcing buildVolume on a test printer. To open the Subdivide dialog, click any button whose innerText matches /Subdivide/ inside ol[data-sonner-toaster]. Subdivide dialog data-testids: subdivide-dialog, subdivide-mode-{auto|manual}, subdivide-auto-{x|y|z}-{value|inc|dec}, subdivide-manual-{x|y|z}-{input|add|chip-N}, subdivide-connector-{none|dowel|dovetail}, subdivide-connector-size, subdivide-expected-pieces, subdivide-apply-btn, subdivide-close. Note: Playwright element.click() can be intercepted by the sonner toast layer when it overlaps the dialog top — prefer document.querySelector(sel).click() via page.evaluate for the +/- steppers. Pieces are named 'Cube/x{n}{a|b}/y{n}{a|b}/z{n}{a|b}'. With 1/1/1 cuts, expect 8 pieces + 12 dowel connectors (when Dowels selected). Connector tag/flag in state.objects was not detected via the `tag==='subdivideConnector'` filter — needs source confirmation.
rca of the issue: No bugs found requiring RCA. Initial oversize toast wasn't appearing in DOM queries on the first attempt because tests ran before the 350ms debounce had elapsed (resolved by waiting >=900ms after addPrimitive+setTransform). Playwright `click(force=True)` on the +/- steppers failed silently when the warning toast layer was over the dialog top region — switching to native DOM click() inside page.evaluate fixed the test; this is a test-tool quirk, not a product bug (verified by screenshot that the buttons are visually unblocked for human users).
